danger_signals.visual_form: Mob henchmen, prison guards in striped uniforms, boss characters resembling film antagonists
danger_signals.meaning: Active threats requiring combat or evasion

danger_signals.visual_form: Player transformation from masked Mask back to pajama-wearing Stanley Ipkiss
danger_signals.meaning: Critical vulnerability state; loss of power and cartoon invulnerability

danger_signals.visual_form: Depletion of special ability resource bar
danger_signals.meaning: Loss of access to powerful transformative attacks (mallet, horn, guns, tornado)

danger_signals.visual_form: Large convoluted level layouts with multiple paths and dead ends
danger_signals.meaning: Environmental hazard through disorientation and spatial complexity

safety_signals.visual_form: Green mask visually on character, cartoonish exaggerated appearance and animations
safety_signals.meaning: Player invulnerability and access to cartoon physics/special abilities
Restoration of Morph meter for special attack capabilities

safety_signals.visual_form: Mask invisibility visual effect during stealth moves
Ipkiss' apartment, bank interior, ritzy nightclub interiors
safety_signals.meaning: Level progression checkpoints and narrative safe zones

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Nightclub ownership and criminal enterprise aesthetic
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Gang henchmen, prison guards in stereotypical striped uniforms, Dorian as final boss (also wearing the mask)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Nightclub setting as faction headquarters; prison level containing faction-aligned guards; high-rent district controlled by mob interests

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Green Loki mask artifact; cartoon boxing gloves; comedic weapons (mallet, horn, tornado)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Ipkiss' apartment as home base; bank where Stanley works as mild-mannered clerk; park and public spaces as neutral territory

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Player appears as pajama-wearing, ordinary-looking Stanley Ipkiss
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Limited movement capabilities; vulnerability to enemy attacks; no access to special cartoon powers

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Exaggerated cartoonish appearance; green mask visually displayed; toon-style animations and proportions
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Access to impossible physics-defying abilities; cartoon invulnerability; rapid ability cycling through Morph meter

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Mob plan ongoing (city under criminal influence)
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Nightclub as central location; presence of henchmen and guards throughout urban areas; prison setting indicating expanded mob control
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemy patrols in all major zones; hostile NPCs in every environment; escalating boss encounters tied to mob hierarchy

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: 16-bit representation of Tina Carlyle (love interest); big band music aesthetic
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Dancing sequence indicating narrative resolution and romance subplot completion

environmental_storytelling.example: Levels structured as journey through Edge City: Ipkiss' apartment → high-rent district → bank → park → prison → nightclub. Each location reinforces story beats and faction presence.
Prison guards in stereotypical striped uniforms visually communicate faction control of that institution and identify as mob-aligned
All major bosses resemble film characters (Mrs. Peenman the landlady, Irv and Burt the repairmen, Dorian as final antagonist), using visual recognition from movie to communicate narrative stakes
Special moves (mallet from alarm clock scene, massive guns from final confrontation, living horn) drawn directly from film scenes, grounding game abilities in narrative context
Large convoluted maze-like levels reflect urban complexity and mob-controlled city's labyrinthine nature; secret background paths offer alternative navigation revealing world depth

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Returns to pajama-wearing form; loses all special abilities; becomes vulnerable civilian
Employs cartoon physics; rapid offensive combos; access to transformative weapons (mallet, horn, guns, tornado); cartoon boxing glove punches as primary attack
Becomes invisible to enemy detection systems; can move freely through enemy-patrolled areas undetected
Regular patrols through urban and institutional zones; simple AI; respond to player presence with basic combat
Also wearing the mask; possesses same cartoon powers as player; represents escalated threat due to criminal intelligence combined with mask abilities

notes: The Mask uses cartoony visual style based on Dark Horse comic book rather than film's live-action aesthetic. This creates a distinct danger/safety dichotomy: cartoon invulnerability when masked vs. vulnerable clerk state when unmasked. Faction identity relies on location recognition (nightclub as mob territory, bank/apartment as protagonist spaces) and enemy costume convention (prison guards). World state communicates primarily through protagonist transformation and environmental progression through mob-controlled Edge City. Game's humor and visual gags adapted from film establish tone and stakes through recognizable source material rather than dark danger signals.
